full_prompt: Design the forward tilt-rotor boom of a VTOL cargo UAV entered in the Air Cargo Challenge 2026 (which serves as the EUROAVIA European VTOL Design Competition for 2026). The aircraft uses the mandated T-MOTOR AT2814 900 kv motor with fixed prop (14 inch diameter x 4.7 inch pitch), 3S LiPo battery (11.1 V nominal), maximum thrust per motor 28 N at full throttle. The design is a quad-tilt-rotor with 4 booms; this scope is one forward boom. The boom tilts from horizontal (forward-flight) to vertical (VTOL hover) via a servo-driven pivot at the wing-root end. Boom length 280 mm, rectangular tube cross-section 22 mm x 15 mm x 1.5 mm wall, carbon-fiber pultruded (E_long 150 GPa, tensile 1800 MPa, compressive 1200 MPa, density 1550 kg/m^3). At the outboard end the boom supports the motor and prop assembly (95 g). At the inboard end the boom pivots through a 25 mm diameter aluminum clevis bearing. The aircraft MTOW is 3.5 kg with 1.5 kg transport payload in a bounding-box 250 x 200 x 150 mm container. Load cases: (LC1) hover: 28 N thrust at motor end, axial along boom; (LC2) forward-flight transition: boom tilted 30 degrees, 22 N thrust forward + 11 N gravity lateral, producing combined bending on boom; (LC3) 1.5 g pull-up limit: 42 N at motor end vertical; (LC4) ultimate 1.5 x LC3 = 63 N per STANAG 4703 composite FoS 1.5; (LC5) transition stall: boom momentarily misaligned during tilt transition, motor thrust 28 N acts at 20 deg misalignment producing unbalanced side load 9.6 N at boom tip; (LC6) hard landing on one boom: 5 g equivalent on 95 g motor mass = 4.6 N at motor end downward, simultaneously with impact reaction on the clevis 4.6 N upward. Pass/fail: (R1) under LC4 ultimate, max composite stress must remain below 900 MPa (1800 / 2.0 FoS) in tension, 600 MPa (1200 / 2.0) in compression; (R2) tip deflection under LC3 limit must remain below 4 mm to preserve prop-fuselage clearance; (R3) boom dry mass must not exceed 45 g; (R4) first cantilever natural frequency must exceed 30 Hz; (R5) clevis bearing stress under LC5 misalignment must remain below 184 MPa (276 / 1.5 yield FoS on 6061-T6 clevis); (R6) linear buckling eigenvalue under LC1 axial thrust must exceed 4.0 to preserve flutter margin. Thermal, fluid, radiation analyses not primary. Deliverables: STEP AP242 boom + clevis assembly (2 bodies), composite stack-up, FEA for each load case, buckling report for LC1, modal, mass.
short_prompt:
Design the forward tilt-rotor boom of a VTOL cargo UAV entered in Air Cargo Challenge 2026 (EUROAVIA European VTOL Design Competition 2026). Mandated T-MOTOR AT2814 900 kv + 14 x 4.7 prop on 3S LiPo, 28 N max thrust per motor. Quad-tilt-rotor with 4 booms; scope is one forward boom tilting horizontal-to-vertical via a servo pivot at the wing-root end. Aircraft MTOW 3.5 kg + 1.5 kg payload in a 250 x 200 x 150 mm bay.

Envelope: pultruded CFRP rectangular tube 22 x 15 mm x 1.5 mm wall x 280 mm, 95 g motor + prop at the outboard end, 25 mm aluminum clevis-bearing at the inboard pivot.

Factor regime: STANAG 4703 composite ultimate FoS 1.5 = FoS 2.0 stated here (tensile 900 MPa / compressive 600 MPa); tip deflection at limit < 4 mm (prop-fuselage clearance); dry mass <= 45 g; first cantilever mode > 30 Hz; clevis yield FoS 1.5 (184 MPa on 6061-T6); linear buckling under axial thrust >= 4.0 (flutter margin).

Load cases.
- Hover: 28 N axial thrust along the boom at the motor end.
- Forward-flight transition (30 deg tilt): 22 N forward + 11 N gravity lateral.
- Limit pull-up 1.5 g: 42 N vertical at the motor end.
- Ultimate 1.5 x limit: 63 N per STANAG 4703.
- Transition-stall side load: 9.6 N at boom tip (28 N at 20 deg misalignment).
- Hard landing on one boom: 4.6 N at the motor end + 4.6 N up at the clevis (5 g on 95 g motor mass).

prompt.title: EUROAVIA Air Cargo Challenge 2026 VTOL Forward Tilt-Rotor Boom
prompt.brief: CFRP pultruded tilt-rotor boom 280 mm for 28 N T-MOTOR AT2814 mandated motor, STANAG 4703 composite FoS.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: beam/shell hybrid for boom; TET10 for clevis

exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Prop wash reduced to thrust vector.
